title: Fabric Interconnect HA Cluster State
spl:
index=cisco_ucs sourcetype="cisco:ucs:fi_stats" object_type="fi_cluster" earliest=-4h
| stats latest(ha_state) as ha, latest(oper_state) as op, latest(cluster_state) as cl by fi_id
| where ha!="up" OR op!="up" OR cl!="healthy"
| table fi_id, ha, op, cl
description: FI pairs provide control-plane and northbound redundancy. Loss of subordinate or split state risks asymmetric forwarding and prolonged change freezes until the fabric is healed.
value: FI pairs provide control-plane and northbound redundancy. Loss of subordinate or split state risks asymmetric forwarding and prolonged change freezes until the fabric is healed.
implementation: (1) Ingest FI cluster state from periodic API or structured syslog; (2) alert on any FI not `up` or cluster not `healthy`; (3) runbook to verify L1/L2 links and UCSM primary/ subordinate roles.
visualization: Table (FI cluster state), Timeline (state transitions), Single value (unhealthy FI count).

references.title: Splunk Lantern тАФ use case library

detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
тАв Install and configure the required add-on or app: `Splunk_TA_cisco-ucs`, UCS Manager syslog.
тАв Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=cisco_ucs` `sourcetype="cisco:ucs:fi_stats"` with fields `fi_id`, `ha_state`, `oper_state`, `cluster_state`.
тАв For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 тАФ Configure data collection
(1) Ingest FI cluster state from periodic API or structured syslog; (2) alert on any FI not `up` or cluster not `healthy`; (3) runbook to verify L1/L2 links and UCSM primary/ subordinate roles.

Step 2 тАФ Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=cisco_ucs sourcetype="cisco:ucs:fi_stats" object_type="fi_cluster" earliest=-4h
| stats latest(ha_state) as ha, latest(oper_state) as op, latest(cluster_state) as cl by fi_id
| where ha!="up" OR op!="up" OR cl!="healthy"
| table fi_id, ha, op, cl
```

Understanding this SPL

**Fabric Interconnect HA Cluster State** тАФ FI pairs provide control-plane and northbound redundancy. Loss of subordinate or split state risks asymmetric forwarding and prolonged change freezes until the fabric is healed.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=cisco_ucs` `sourcetype="cisco:ucs:fi_stats"` with fields `fi_id`, `ha_state`, `oper_state`, `cluster_state`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): `Splunk_TA_cisco-ucs`, UCS Manager syslog. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feedтАФrename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: cisco_ucs; **sourcetype**: cisco:ucs:fi_stats. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

тАв Scopes the data: index=cisco_ucs, sourcetype="cisco:ucs:fi_stats", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
тАв `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by fi_id** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
тАв Filters the current rows with `where ha!="up" OR op!="up" OR cl!="healthy"` тАФ typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
тАв Pipeline stage (see **Fabric Interconnect HA Cluster State**): table fi_id, ha, op, cl


Step 3 тАФ Validate
Compare with Cisco UCS Manager (faults, equipment, and service profile context) for the same DNs and time range.

Step 4 тАФ Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (FI cluster state), Timeline (state transitions), Single value (unhealthy FI count).
grandmaExplanation: FI pairs provide control-plane and northbound redundancy тАФ so hardware faults and capacity problems do not take services down.
knownFalsePositives: Cluster events during planned hardware refresh, capacity adds, or scheduled maintenance modes.
